questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: The 'Igla-S' system, recently in the news, is primarily designed for which of the following operational roles?

options.text: Air defense for mobile ground units in high-threat environments

questions.explanation: The Igla-S is a man-portable surface-to-air missile system specifically designed for mobile ground units to provide air defense against aerial threats in high-threat environments.
questions.takeaway: Igla-S is a tactical air defense tool for ground forces.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: It is a man-portable, shoulder-launched surface-to-air missile (SAM) system designed for mobile ground units operating in high-threat environments.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In the context of air defense architecture, how does the Igla-S system differ from Medium Range Surface-to-Air Missile (MRSAM) systems?

options.text: Igla-S is a shoulder-launched system for point defense, whereas MRSAM is typically vehicle-mounted for area defense.
Igla-S uses radar-based guidance, while MRSAM relies exclusively on infrared heat-seeking technology.
Igla-S is designed for exo-atmospheric interception, while MRSAM is restricted to the lower troposphere.
Igla-S is an offensive air-to-air missile, whereas MRSAM is a defensive surface-to-air system.

questions.explanation: The Igla-S is a man-portable (MANPADS), shoulder-launched system (VSHORADS) meant for short-range point defense. In contrast, Medium Range systems (MRSAM) are larger, vehicle-mounted, and provide defense over a wider area. Option B is incorrect as Igla-S uses IR homing. Option C is incorrect as Igla-S is limited to 3.5 km altitude. Option D is incorrect as both are surface-to-air systems.
questions.takeaway: MANPADS like Igla-S are distinguished by their portability and short-range 'point defense' role compared to larger SAM batteries.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: It is a man-portable, shoulder-launched surface-to-air missile (SAM) system designed for mobile ground units operating in high-threat environments. It is an improved version of Very Short Range Air Defence Systems (VSHORADS).
